skill.name: airtable-automation
skill.description: Automates Airtable record, schema, field, view, and comment workflows through the Rube MCP Airtable toolkit.
skill.summary: Use Rube MCP tools to inspect Airtable bases and safely manage records, fields, and comments.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are false positives from markdown code formatting and ordinary Airtable terms. The real risk is operational: the skill directs users to connect a third-party MCP endpoint and run Airtable tools that can read, create, update, and delete workspace data.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Access to Airtable Data
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add the Rube MCP endpoint and authenticate Airtable. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it grants an external integration access to Airtable data and automation actions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly directs users to configure an external MCP endpoint and complete Airtable authentication. This confirms third-party network and data-access risk, but not malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Airtable Modification and Deletion Workflows
medium_findings.description: The skill includes workflows for creating, updating, and deleting Airtable records and modifying schema metadata. These are expected capabilities, but mistakes can alter or remove production data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented tool sequences directly include create, update, delete, and schema update operations. The risk is operational and permission-dependent rather than evidence of malware.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are inline markdown references to MCP tool names, parameters, Airtable formulas, or identifiers. No executable Ruby code, shell command, or script body is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows backticks used only for markdown inline code and documentation examples. There is no command execution context in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are Substring False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts at the reviewed lines match ordinary Airtable documentation text such as description and update field references. No hash, cipher, crypto API, or credential handling code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain descriptive Airtable workflow text, not cryptographic implementation. The scanner appears to have matched short substrings inside benign words.

dangerous_patterns.title: External MCP Endpoint Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs users to configure https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server. This network dependency should be disclosed because it mediates access to Airtable operations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicitly documented as the required MCP server endpoint. This is an intentional integration pattern, not hidden exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Manual Airtable updates are slow and error-prone when bases contain many records or schema details. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP Airtable tools for structured automation.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for Rube MCP and Airtable authentication.
Lists Airtable bases and retrieves base schema before changes.
Creates, reads, updates, and deletes Airtable records through MCP tools.
Builds record searches with Airtable filter formulas and pagination.
Documents field, table, and comment management workflows.
Calls out Airtable limits for batch size, page size, and rate limits.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP to be configured in the user client.
Requires an active Airtable connection with suitable permissions.
Does not include standalone scripts or direct Airtable API code.
Cannot prevent data loss if users approve destructive Airtable actions.

use_cases.title: Update Campaign Records
use_cases.description: Marketing teams can filter Airtable records by status and update campaign fields in controlled batches.

use_cases.title: Prepare Base Schema Changes
use_cases.description: Product teams can inspect table structure and plan field additions before modifying shared bases.

use_cases.title: Audit Database Content
use_cases.description: Support teams can list records, review comments, and find stale items using Airtable formulas.

prompt_templates.title: List Available Bases
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Airtable automation skill to check my Rube MCP Airtable connection, then list the bases I can access.

prompt_templates.title: Find Matching Records
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the base schema, then find records in the Tasks table where Status is Active. Return the matching record names and IDs.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Update Records
prompt_templates.prompt: Find up to 30 records where Priority is greater than 1 and Status is Backlog. Propose batch updates in groups of 10 before applying them.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Schema Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the base schema, identify fields needed for a new intake workflow, and draft a safe Airtable field creation plan with validation steps.

output_examples.input: Find active onboarding records and show the next action.
output_examples.output: A concise table of matching records with record ID, owner, due date, current status, and recommended next action.

output_examples.input: Create records for a new event checklist.
output_examples.output: A confirmation plan that groups new records into Airtable-safe batches and asks for approval before writing data.

output_examples.input: Review the schema for missing support fields.
output_examples.output: A schema review summary with existing fields, missing fields, field type suggestions, and risks before changes are made.

best_practices: Search Rube tools first so the current Airtable schemas are used.
Inspect base schema before filtering records or writing field values.
Ask for confirmation before updates, deletes, or schema changes.
anti_patterns: Do not run delete operations without record IDs and user confirmation.
Do not assume field names because Airtable formulas are case-sensitive.
Do not change filters or sorting while paginating through records.

faq.question: Does this skill connect directly to Airtable?
faq.answer: No. It guides Airtable work through the Rube MCP and Composio Airtable toolkit.

faq.question: Do I need an Airtable account?
faq.answer: Yes. You need an Airtable account and an active authenticated connection through Rube MCP.

faq.question: Can it create or update records?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents create, update, list, filter, and delete record workflows.

faq.question: Can it change table fields?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes field creation and metadata update workflows where Airtable permissions allow them.

faq.question: Why does it say to search tools first?
faq.answer: Rube MCP tool schemas can change. Searching first helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use current parameters.

faq.question: Is it safe for production bases?
faq.answer: Use it carefully. Review schema, preview affected records, and confirm any write or delete action before execution.
